lede: A look at October 5 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Denis Diderot and Reinhard Selten, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Mekong River massacre
events.desc: In the Mekong River massacre, two Chinese cargo boats are hijacked and 13 crew members murdered.

events.title: Ladbroke Grove rail crash
events.desc: The Ladbroke Grove rail crash in West London kills 31 people.

events.title: Order of the Solar Temple
events.desc: Swiss police find the bodies of 48 members of the Order of the Solar Temple, who had died in a cult mass murder-suicide.

events.title: Augusto Pinochet
events.desc: A Chilean opposition coalition defeats Augusto Pinochet in his re-election attempt.

events.title: Commercial Import Program
events.desc: The United States suspends the Commercial Import Program in response to repression of the Buddhist majority by the regime of President Ngo Dinh Diem.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Pacific Theater: Ninety-eight American POWs are executed by Japanese forces on Wake Island.

events.title: Jarrow March
events.desc: The Jarrow March sets off for London.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: An aircraft successfully destroys another aircraft with gunfire for the first time.

events.title: Eastman tunnel
events.desc: The Eastman tunnel, in Minnesota, United States, collapses during construction, causing a landslide that nearly destroys St. Anthony Falls.

events.title: Fourth Council of Constantinople (Catholic Church)
events.desc: The Fourth Council of Constantinople is convened to depose patriarch Photios I.

births.name: Reinhard Selten
births.line: German economist and mathematician, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Chester A. Arthur
births.line: American general, lawyer, and politician, 21st President of the United States

births.name: Václav Havel
births.line: Czech poet, playwright, and politician, 1st President of the Czech Republic

births.name: Fritz Fischer (medical doctor)
births.line: German physician and convicted war criminal

deaths.text: Ruth R. Benerito, American chemist and academic
Carlo Lizzani, Italian actor, director, producer, and screenwriter
Steve Jobs, American businessman, co-founder of Apple Inc.

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Francis Xavier Seelos (Roman Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Hor and Susia (Coptic Orthodox Church of Alexandria)
